headline: Stock Futures Skid While Oil Prices Surge
summary: Oil prices surged and global stock markets slumped after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran showed signs of sparking a widening conflict across the Middle East.

headline: Lockheed and RTX Soar. How Defense Stocks Stand to Gain—and Lose—From the U.S. Attacks on Iran
summary: The U.S. attack on Iran promises to increase already-high geopolitical tensions, which have prompted countries around the world to raise spending on national defense.  Lockheed Martin  was up 6.9% in premarket trading Monday, while RTX gained 7.1%.

headline: U.S. Defense Stocks Surge Premarket
summary: U.S. defense stocks surged premarket as markets reacted to escalating conflict across the Middle East after the U.S. and Israel launched a wave of attacks against Iran over the weekend. Futures tied to Lockheed Martin jumped 7.

headline: Middle East Tensions Put RTX Valuation And New Defense Contracts In Focus
summary: RTX (NYSE:RTX) is drawing fresh attention as renewed conflict across the Middle East focuses governments and investors on defense capabilities and procurement needs. The company recently secured new high-profile government contracts, including work on DARPA's XENA program for advanced situational awareness and supply deals for customized optics to the German Armed Forces. RTX, which combines missile systems, avionics, cybersecurity and defense electronics, sits at the center of many...

headline: Lockheed Martin stock rises on Iran conflict, Nvidia invests $2B
summary: Morning Brief host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Monday morning's trending tickers and stories. Defense stocks, like Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC), are surging on the latest military escalation in Iran. Meanwhile, the Iran conflict is causing airline stocks to plunge, including American (AAL), Delta (DAL), and United (UAL). Nvidia (NVDA) will announce $2 billion in Lumentum's (LITE) artificial intelligence (AI) research and development. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

content.title: Lockheed Martin stock rises on Iran conflict, Nvidia invests $2B
content.description:
Morning Brief host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Monday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Defense stocks, like Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC), are surging on the latest military escalation in Iran.

Meanwhile, the Iran conflict is causing airline stocks to plunge, including American (AAL), Delta (DAL), and United (UAL).

Nvidia (NVDA) will announce $2 billion in Lumentum's (LITE) artificial intelligence (AI) research and development.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

headline: Three U.S. F-15 Jets Shot Down by Kuwait. Everything to Know About Them.
summary: Three U.S. F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jets were mistakenly shot down on Sunday night by Kuwaiti forces amid the continuing conflict with Iran.  “All six aircrew ejected safely, have been safely recovered, and are in stable condition,” said U.S. Central Command in a news release.  “Kuwait has acknowledged this incident, and we are grateful for the efforts of the Kuwaiti defense forces and their support in this ongoing operation.”

content.title: Three U.S. F-15 Jets Shot Down by Kuwait. Everything to Know About Them.
content.summary: Three U.S. F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jets were mistakenly shot down on Sunday night by Kuwaiti forces amid the continuing conflict with Iran.  “All six aircrew ejected safely, have been safely recovered, and are in stable condition,” said U.S. Central Command in a news release.  “Kuwait has acknowledged this incident, and we are grateful for the efforts of the Kuwaiti defense forces and their support in this ongoing operation.”

headline: China's Rare Earth Grip on the U.S. Military Is About to Break
summary: In a typical Chinese rare earth processing plant, 200 workers move through a maze of massive chemical tanks, risking life and limb to produce the materials that power everything from fighter jets and missile components to cellphones. Hundreds of these facilities operate across China, and they give Beijing overwhelming control over the single most critical choke point in the modern industrial economy. Companies mentioned in this release include: REalloys Inc. (ALOY), Lockheed Martin Corporation (

headline: Lockheed Martin, Other Defense Stocks Fire Up After Iran Strikes
summary: Defense stocks rallied Monday after the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.  In the U.S., shares of Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX) and Northrop Grumman (NOC) all gained at least 3% Monday morning.  In Europe, U.K.-listed BAE Systems gained more than 4%.

content.title: Lockheed Martin, Other Defense Stocks Fire Up After Iran Strikes
content.summary: Defense stocks rallied Monday after the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.  In the U.S., shares of Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX) and Northrop Grumman (NOC) all gained at least 3% Monday morning.  In Europe, U.K.-listed BAE Systems gained more than 4%.

headline: A Look At RTX (RTX) Valuation As Geopolitical Tensions And New Defense Contracts Lift Expectations
summary: RTX (RTX) is back in focus after fresh U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran pushed defense stocks sharply higher, with traders linking the move to expectations for increased demand for air and missile defense systems. See our latest analysis for RTX. The latest move comes on top of a strong run, with RTX posting a 20.0% 90 day share price return and a 54.0% one year total shareholder return. Multi year contracts like the DARPA XENA program and new optics orders in Europe have also kept interest...
